svg.title: Markup-Language Family Tree (Mission SCRIBE T1)
svg.desc: Cladogram showing the genealogy of document markup languages. Two parallel branches: procedural formatters (RUNOFF, SCRIPT, troff, TeX, LaTeX) on the left, and generic-markup (Tunnicliffe 1967, GenCode, GML, SGML, HTML, XML and the XML family) on the right. Cross-branch edges show known design influences: SCRIBE influencing LaTeX (1985), TeX-math feeding into MathML pipelines, Markdown later transcompiling to HTML, Pandoc bridging both branches.
svg.text: Mission SCRIBE · Track 1 MARKUP-LINEAGE · doc 06 cladogram

g.text: Bosak, Bray, Sperberg-McQueen, Paoli, Clark · W3C · 1998

svg.text: TeX-source feeds MathML rendering pipelines (KaTeX, MathJax, Temml, LaTeXML)
Pandoc bridges procedural ↔ generic branches via shared AST
Hand-authored native SVG. Mission SCRIBE v1.49.621 · 2026-05-09. Self-demo bar artifact for T1 doc 06.
